fact: Self-improvement project created as structured reflection system. Uses AGENTS.md policy for session-start memory loading, .learnings/ directory for corrections/errors/requests, and MEMORY.md for curated durable knowledge.

Morning Meeting Protocol established: Pre-check nightly-memory-extraction, then review security/health/self-improvement reports in order. Process each finding: present problem+risk+fix, wait for decision, log decisions in daily memory, suppress repeat accepted-risk findings.

Self-improvement workflow fully operational (Mar 6): Corrections logged to .learnings/corrections.md, errors to .learnings/errors.md, requests to .learnings/requests.md. Session-end reminder added to AGENTS.md.

March 12 self-improvement changes applied: Added .learnings reminder to session-end in AGENTS.md, timezone display vs runtime clarified in TOOLS.md, SUBAGATE-POLICY clarified for Codex non-trivial coding, HEARTBEAT queue reference fixed, cron table updated with dependency-update-audit and skill-level-check.

Duplicate prevention hardening: Three-layer fix applied to autonomous executor - (1) queue hardening: duplicate detection uses normalized task text + deliverable paths, (2) pre-selection prune: already-satisfied backlog items skipped before scoring, (3) prune visibility: executor log now surfaces pruned tasks with verified output paths.

Self-reflection captured: When splitting governance policy, also wire at least one enforcement mechanism right away and run validation pass, because live execution exposes reliability edges faster than document review.
Self-reflection captured: When preventing duplicate autonomous work, put guards at the earliest cheap decision point first, then reinforce downstream so recovery works if upstream state drifts.
